sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root שָׁמָר)-

(1) custody, guard.-(a) i.q. prison, Gen 40:3, seqq.; 42:17 .-(b) station of a watch, Neh 7:3 Jer 51:12 hence used of the watchmen themselves, Neh 4:3, 16 Job 7:12.-(c) that which is guarded. Pro 4:23, “keep thy heart מִכָּל־מִשְׁמָר above all the things which are to be guarded.”

(2) observance, that which is observed, rite, Neh 13:14. Concr. one who is observed, to whom reverence is due, used of a ruler, Eze 38:7.
